title: "podman ps" and "podman info" hang indefinitiely (while podman is starting some container)
body:
### Issue Description

Running `podman info` or `podman ps` results in an indefnite hang.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Basically the same system with the problem as in https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/23504 aka `podman-compose` is used to start multiple services at boot via a systemd unit. Even if that is disabled and rebooted, still have that issue.

It only fails **for this user**, other users using podman on the same system it works. Maybe because some container is still initializing/running?

### Describe the results you received

```
$  podman --log-level=debug ps
```

### Describe the results you expected

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

```
$ rpm-ostree status -b
State: idle
AutomaticUpdatesDriver: Zincati
  DriverState: active; periodically polling for updates (last checked Sat 2026-01-24 15:08:40 UTC)
BootedDeployment:
● ostree-image-signed:docker://quay.io/fedora/fedora-coreos:stable
                   Digest: sha256:688fb50c9f19fecb638f660d2ad66fcafe47cb31d7a3cef98370dbde4d3315e9
                  Version: 43.20251214.3.0 (2026-01-02T19:17:54Z)
```

Also tried with previous Fedora CoreOS v43.20260105.3.0

### Additional information

I had this issue in the past when the containers were starting and it was kinda delayed.
But there I could at some point kill it.

**Edit:** Ah just saw multiple `podman start nextcloud_nc` comands in `htop`, it was I guess still starting the Nextcloud image. Anyway bad to get no context/information about this while it is starting.

```
$ podman version
WARN[0000] The deprecated BoltDB database driver is in use. This driver will be removed in the upcoming Podman 6.0 release in mid 2026. It is advised that you migrate to SQLite to avoid issues when this occurs. Set SUPPRESS_BOLTDB_WARNING environment variable to remove this message. 
Client:        Podman Engine
Version:       5.7.1
API Version:   5.7.1
Go Version:    go1.25.4 X:nodwarf5
Git Commit:    f845d14e941889ba4c071f35233d09b29d363c75
Built:         Wed Dec 10 01:00:00 2025
Build Origin:  Fedora Project
OS/Arch:       linux/amd64
```

**Edit:** I guess this is because Nextcloud has a long-running startup process and updated the version with plugins etc.

comments.body: we've done some improvements in this area but I cannot remember when they landed.  @mheon do you recall when those locking improvements, i think @Luap99 did them, landed?

without actual information on how the container are created and started it is impossible to tell. The container process itself should not matter and/or cause hangs as we do not hold locks once the process is running.

If there are still podman start processes then they hold the container lock during the start process and as such it is expected that all other commands who list all containers hang as they need to wait for that lock. There are improvements that could be made overall in our locking model and I would still like to work on that at some point but for now this seems like expected behavior.

So the only question is why does podman start take so long? On of of main systems I have a one TB volume with data files attached that use :Z mount and as such the files get relabelled for a long time during startup which blocks things for minutes for example but I am sure there can be other slow things inside the startup. 

To know if there is really a container lock held there is the `podman system locks` command that can be used while other commands hang.

The image being used/likely affecting this is the https://github.com/nextcloud/docker one, You can just pull a default one (in my case the `fpm` one) and it should be reproducible. If not, best idea is to pull a major older version and then upgrade the image/container to a newer one.

A full example should also be this compose (running with `podman-compose`): https://github.com/nextcloud/docker?tab=readme-ov-file#base-version---fpm

> I have a one TB volume with data files attached that use :Z mount and as such the files get relabelled for a long time during startup which blocks things for minutes for example but I am sure there can be other slow things inside the startup.

Also using :Z here.

> So the only question is why does podman start take so long?

My guess was because the entrypoint script in the container took so long to upgrade all Nextcloud plugins?

> > So the only question is why does podman start take so long?
> 
> My guess was because the entrypoint script in the container took so long to upgrade all Nextcloud plugins?

No start exits right away normally, we don't know what your container process does or not so a slow container startup doesn't matter. The only special case is `--sdnotify healthy` which would make podman block until the container healthcheck gets ready but that does not hold the lock for that full duration because well the healthcheck command itself would not be able to get the lock then.



> > I have a one TB volume with data files attached that use :Z mount and as such the files get relabelled for a long time during startup which blocks things for minutes for example but I am sure there can be other slow things inside the startup.
> 
> Also using :Z here.

Well then how big is your volume? If you have many files that is expected to take a while and I don't see a way to avoid that.


The other thing to consider is that if running rootless and using custom userns mappings we have to copy and chown the full image on creation which is really slow and holds the store locks for a really long time, see https://github.com/containers/container-libs/issues/506
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
